title: Known-Answer Tests, Negative Vectors, Fuzzing, and Interoperability · Go Semantics Case: Crypto Go Negative Mutation Tests
summary: A library has only round-trip tests, generates random fixtures on every run, never mutates tags or headers, and calls 100 percent line coverage cryptographic assurance. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the negative mutation tests increment in layered cryptographic verification harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Mutate every authenticated field, key, nonce, tag, length, order, version, context, and boundary and require clean rejection without partial effects.
Use authoritative known-answer and protocol vectors with provenance, exact bytes, expected successes, expected failures, and stable fixtures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
